name: rda-toolbox
documentation:
# Robotic-assisted Discovery of Antiinfectives

This package aims to provide a toolbox for data analysis in the field of drug discovery.

---

The aim is to provide functions to help evaluate the following assays:
- Primary Screen
- MIC (Minimum Inhibitory Concentration) Assay
- Cellviability

### Usage
`pip install rda-toolbox`

or

`pip install "git+https://github.com/Robotic-Discovery-of-Antiinfectives/rda-toolbox.git"`


```Python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import rda_toolbox as rda
import glob

rda.readerfiles_rawdf(glob.glob("path/to/raw/readerfiles/*"))
```


### File Parsing
- Read output files and return readouts in a [tidy](https://r4ds.had.co.nz/tidy-data.html), [long](https://towardsdatascience.com/long-and-wide-formats-in-data-explained-e48d7c9a06cb) DataFrame

#### **Supported readers:**
- Cytation C10

### Plotting
This package uses [Vega-Altair](https://altair-viz.github.io/index.html) for creating interactive (or static) visualizations.

- Plate Heatmaps
- Upset plots
  - `UpSetAltair` plotting function is taken from https://github.com/hms-dbmi/upset-altair-notebook and modified
  - This part of this package is licensed under MIT license.
<!-- https://testdriven.io/blog/python-project-workflow/ -->


### New Release
1) Update `pyproject.toml` release version
2) Update `docs/source/conf.py` release version
3) On GitHub go to *releases* and `Draft a new release`

modules.name: rda_toolbox
documentation: expose functions here to be able to:

functions.name: rda_toolbox.marimo.readeruploads_rawdf
documentation:
Wrapper function to collect the results from marimos file upload button.
This function is similar to readerfiles_rawdf() except that the input
is a list of marimo.ui.file() outputs instead of a list of filepaths.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.get_rows_cols
documentation: Obtain number of rows and columns as tuple for corresponding plate type.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.generate_inputtable
documentation:
Generates an input table for the corresponding readout dataframe.
If not readout df is provided, create a minimal input df.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.map_96_to_384
documentation:
Maps the rows and columns of 4 96-Well plates into a single 384-Well plate.

- Maps in Z order.
- Takes row, column and quadrant (each of the 96-well plates is one quadrant) of a well from 4 96-well plates and maps it to the corresponding well in a 384-well plate

Returns the 384-Well plate row and column.
Example: `df["Row_384"], df["Col_384"] = zip(*df.apply(map_96_to_384, axis=1))`

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.mapapply_96_to_384
documentation:
Apply to a DataFrame the mapping of 96-well positions to 384-well positions.

- Maps in Z order.
The DataFrame has to have columns with:
- 96-well plate row positions
- 96-well plate column positions
- 96-well plate to 384-well plate quadrants
*(4 96-well plates fit into 1 384-well plate)*

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.split_position
documentation:
Split a position like "A1" into row and column positions ("A", 1) and adds them as columns to the DataFrame.
Hint: Remove NAs before applying this function. E.g. `split_position(df.dropna(subset="Position"))`

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.get_selection
documentation:
Apply this ahead of get_upsetplot_df (to obtain dummies df).
After all the above, apply UpSetAltair.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.get_upsetplot_df
documentation:
Function to obtain a correctly formatted DataFrame.
According to [UpSetR-shiny](https://github.com/hms-dbmi/UpSetR-shiny)
this table is supposed to be encoded in binary and set up so that each column represents a set, and each row represents an element.
If an element is in the set it is represented as a 1 in that position. If an element is not in the set it is represented as a 0.

*Thanks to: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37381862/get-dummies-for-pandas-column-containing-list*

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.chunks
documentation:
Useful function if you want to put a certain amount
of observations into one plot.
Yield n number of striped chunks from l.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.mic_assaytransfer_mapping
documentation: This is a rather unfinished function to map 96 well motherplates to 384 well assay transfer (AsT) plates.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.imgbuffer_to_imgstr
documentation: Encode imagebuffer to string (default base64-encoded string). Example: imgbuffer_to_imgstr(mol_to_bytes(mol)), prefix="'")

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.smiles_to_imgstr
documentation:
Converts a smiles string to a base64 encoded image string (e.g. for plotting in altair).
It's a convenience function consisting of rda.utility.mol_to_bytes() and rda.utility.imgbuffer_to_imgstr(),
use these if you want more fine grained control over the format of the returned string.
Example: df["image"] = df["smiles"].apply(lambda smiles: smiles_to_imgstr(smiles))

functions.name: rda_toolbox.utility.prepare_visualization
documentation: Does formatting for the facet lineplots.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.process.background_normalize_zfactor
documentation:
This function is supposed to be applied to a grouped DataFrame.
It does the following operations:
- Background subtraction by subtracting the mean of the blanks per plate
- Normalization by applying max-normalization using the 'Negative Controls'
- Z-Factor calculation using negative controls and blanks

*`negative_controls` are controls with organism (e.g. bacteria) and medium*
*and are labeled in the input DataFrame as 'Negative Controls'.*
*`blanks` are controls with only medium and are labeled*
*in the input DataFrame as 'Medium'.*

functions.name: rda_toolbox.process.preprocess
documentation:
- raw_df: raw reader data obtained with `rda.readerfiles_rawdf()`
- input_df: input specifications table with required columns:
    - Dataset (with specified references as their own dataset 'Reference')
    - ID (substance_id) (with specified blanks and negative_controls)
    - Assay Transfer Barcode
    - Row_384 (or Row_96)
    - Col_384 (or Col_96)
    - Concentration
    - Replicate (specifying replicate number)
    - Organism (scientific organism name i.e. with strain)
---
Processing function which merges raw reader data (raw_df)
with input specifications table (input_df) and then
normalizes, calculates Z-Factor per plate (norm_by_barcode)
and rounds to sensible decimal places.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.process.get_thresholded_subset
documentation:
WARNING: DEPRECATED, use apply_threshold() instead.
Expects a DataFrame with a mic_cutoff column

functions.name: rda_toolbox.process.mic_process_inputs
documentation: Applies provided threshold to processed data.
Else provide Cutoff via 'threshold' keyword argument.
Returns a smaller DataFrame than was given via input.
# Use only substance entries, no controls, no blanks etc.:
if threshold: # overwrite possibly provided cutoff via input df
"No threshold argument provided and no 'Cutoff' column in Input.xlsx" +
# highest conc. needs to be below the threshold
# measurement at any conc. below threshold
# filter for groups where the measurement at max. conc. is below the given threshold
filter(lambda x: x not in non_grouping_columns, selection.columns)
{key: [value] for key, value in zip(grouping_columns, grp_columns)}

functions.name: rda_toolbox.process.mic_results
documentation:
Expects the results from rda.preprocess() function.
Means measurements between replicates and obtains the MIC values per substance and organism.
Saves excel files per dataset and sheets per organism with Minimum Inhibitory Concentrations (MICs)
at the given thresholds.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.plot.plateheatmaps
documentation:
- df: Dataframe with relevant data
- ID: column name in df containing the unique substance id
- measurement: column name in df with the measurements to colorize via heatmaps
- negative_control: controls with organism + medium
- blank: controls with only medium (no organism and therefore no growth)
Plots heatmaps of the plates from df in a gridlike manner.
Exclude unwanted plates, for example Blanks from the df outside this function, like so
`df[df["Organism"] != "Blank"]`
before plotting, otherwise it will appear as an extra plate.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.plot.upsetaltair_top_level_configuration
documentation: The following two functions are (modified versions) from https://github.com/hms-dbmi/upset-altair-notebook
and were provided under the MIT licence
by Sehi L'yi and Nils Gehlenborg

functions.name: rda_toolbox.plot.UpSetAltair
documentation:
This function generates Altair-based interactive UpSet plots.

Parameters:
    - data (pandas.DataFrame): Tabular data containing the membership of each element (row) in
        exclusive intersecting sets (column).
    - sets (list): List of set names of interest to show in the UpSet plots.
        This list reflects the order of sets to be shown in the plots as well.
    - abbre (dict): Dictionary mapping set names to abbreviated set names.
    - sort_by (str): "frequency" or "degree"
    - sort_by_order (str): "ascending" or "descending"
    - inter_degree_frequency: "ascending" or "descending", only makes sense if sort_by="degree"
    - width (int): Vertical size of the UpSet plot.
    - height (int): Horizontal size of the UpSet plot.
    - height_ratio (float): Ratio of height between upper and under views, ranges from 0 to 1.
    - horizontal_bar_chart_width (int): Width of horizontal bar chart on the bottom-right.
    - set_colors_dict (dict): Dictionary containing the sets as keys with corresponding colors as values
    - highlight_color (str): Color to encode intersecting sets upon mouse hover.
    - glyph_size (int): Size of UpSet glyph (⬤).
    - set_label_bg_size (int): Size of label background in the horizontal bar chart.
    - line_connection_size (int): width of lines in matrix view.
    - horizontal_bar_size (int): Height of bars in the horizontal bar chart.
    - vertical_bar_label_size (int): Font size of texts in the vertical bar chart on the top.
    - vertical_bar_padding (int): Gap between a pair of bars in the vertical bar charts.
    - set_labelstyle (str): "normal" (default) or "italic"

Run rda.utility.get_upsetplot_df() on the df before trying this function.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.plot.lineplots_facet
documentation:
Assay: MIC
Input: processed_df
Output: Altair Chart with faceted lineplots.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.readerfile_parser
documentation: Parser for files created by the BioTek Cytation C10 Confocal Imaging Reader.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.filepaths_to_filedicts
documentation:
Wrapper function to obtain a list of dictionaries which contain the raw files information like

- different entries of metadata
    - Plate Type
    - Barcode
    - Date
    - Time
    - etc.
- Raw Optical Density (DataFrame)
- Concentration (DataFrame)
- Layout (DataFrame)

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.collect_metadata
documentation: Helperfunction to collect the metadata from all reader files into a dataframe.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.collect_results
documentation: Collect and merge results from the readerfiles.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.parse_readerfiles
documentation:
Wrapper for readerfiles_rawdf to keep backwards compatibility.
Improves readerfiles_rawdf, provide a single path for convenience.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.readerfiles_rawdf
documentation:
Parses data from files declared by filepaths and merges the results into a DataFrame
\:param paths: A list of filepaths corresponding to the raw reader files generated by Cytation10
\:type paths: list[str]
\:return: A DataFrame in tidy and long format with the raw readerfile contents
\:rtype: pd.DataFrame

````
Example

```Python
import glob

source:9: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.

source:9: (WARNING/2) Inline interpreted text or phrase reference start-string without end-string.

rawdata_df = readerfiles_rawdf(glob.glob("path/to/raw/files/*"))
```

source:12: (WARNING/2) Inline emphasis start-string without end-string.

source:12: (WARNING/2) Inline literal start-string without end-string.

source:12: (WARNING/2) Inline interpreted text or phrase reference start-string without end-string.
````

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.readerfiles_metadf
documentation: Parses metadata from files declared by filepaths and merges the results into a DataFrame.

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.process_inputfile
documentation:
Read Input excel file which should have the following columns:
    - Barcode
    - Organism
    - Row_384
    - Col_384
    - ID
Optional columns:
    - Concentration in mg/mL (or other units)
    - Cutoff

functions.name: rda_toolbox.parser.read_platemapping
documentation: Reads a mappingfile generated by the barcode reader.
